prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ONLY_CWT_rf', seasonality_champion='FT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: The validation analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.86) and very high seasonality variance (1.09), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, NaiveSeasonal) differ from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ETS, THETA), suggesting per-horizon selection could help. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses.

Early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, NaiveSeasonal) differ from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ETS, THETA), suggesting per-horizon selection could help. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses."

raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=9 indicates high horizon heterogeneity, favoring per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=0.86 and seasonality_corr_variance=1.087 suggest DBA combination is optimal (pattern_analyst_insights.recommended_method_hint). best_per_horizon_by_validation captures early/late horizon specialists (DWT_rf, NaiveSeasonal vs ETS, THETA). topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 provides stability with few windows (n_windows=3). inverse_rmse_weights_k5_sh0.35 adds weighted diversity with shrinkage to mitigate overfitting."
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.86) and very high seasonality variance (1.09), indicating models capture patterns very differently. Early horizon specialists (DWT_rf, NaiveSeasonal) differ from late horizon specialists (NaiveSeasonal, ETS, THETA), suggesting per-horizon selection could help. Given the high disagreement and seasonality variance, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses.
